figures.caption: Fig. 1: Comparison of detection accuracy and model efficiency on the UniversalFakeDetect benchmark. (a) Efficiency comparison in terms of number of parameters. (b) Mean accuracy across 19 generative models.
Fig. 4: t-SNE visualization of SPARK-IL RAG embedding distributions for real (green) and fake (red) samples across different generative models.

Fig. 2: SPARK-IL architecture. Dual spectral paths process ViT features and RGB pixels through multi-band KAN-FFT blocks. Cross-attention fuses the spectral embeddings, which are used for classification during training and stored in Milvus for retrieval-based inference.

Fig. 3: Multi-band KAN-FFT block. Input features are transformed via FFT, converted to log-magnitude spectrum, partitioned into four frequency bands, processed through dedicated KAN layers, and fused into a spectral embedding.
